Title: New Client Onboarding Copilot
ICBMemberType: Sole trader bookkeeper / BAS agent in practice
WhyItMatters: Onboarding a new bookkeeping client involves collecting software access, prior financials, ABN details, payroll setup, and signing an engagement letter — across 6–10 back-and-forth emails. A sole-trader bookkeeper running 20+ clients can spend half a day per new client just on admin. This agent structures the whole intake into one guided flow.
Description: Guides a new bookkeeping client through the full onboarding intake — collecting business details, software access, prior period information, payroll setup, and scope agreement — and produces a completed engagement summary for the bookkeeper to review.
AUContext: AU-specific: Engagement letter must reference Tax Agent Services Act 2009 (TASA) obligations. Scope must clearly define BAS agent services vs bookkeeping services to avoid TPB compliance issues. ABN validation via ABN Lookup API. Super fund choice form obligations under SG Act.
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: Microsoft Forms collects client business name, ABN, structure (sole trader/company/trust), accounting software, payroll details, prior BAS status. Power Automate validates ABN via HTTP connector (ABN Lookup API). Generative AI node drafts engagement summary. Outputs to SharePoint client folder.
Connectors: Microsoft Forms connector, HTTP connector (ABN Lookup API), SharePoint connector, Generative AI prompt node, Outlook connector
UpsellPath: Add DocuSign connector for electronic engagement letter signature → Orchestration Agent (5pts)

Title: Client Document Chaser Agent
ICBMemberType: BAS agent / bookkeeper with multiple clients
WhyItMatters: The single biggest time drain for a sole-trader bookkeeper is chasing clients for missing receipts, bank statements, and payroll information before BAS lodgement. ICB's own survey flagged this as a leading cause of member burnout. This agent automates the entire chase sequence so the bookkeeper can focus on the work, not the follow-up.
Description: Monitors a SharePoint client register for outstanding documents ahead of BAS or payroll deadlines and sends tiered, personalised email reminders to clients — gentle at 14 days out, firm at 7 days, urgent at 3 days.
AUContext: AU-specific: BAS lodgement deadlines are quarterly (28th of month following quarter end, or 25th for BAS agents with lodgement program). Payroll cut-off is set by the bookkeeper. Privacy Act 1988 applies to client financial information — reminders must not expose other clients' details.
BuildNotes: SharePoint list: client name, BAS quarter, deadline date, documents required, documents received (checklist). Power Automate recurrence checks outstanding items. Condition branches on days remaining: 14 days → Outlook reminder 1, 7 days → reminder 2 (firmer tone), 3 days → urgent. Generative AI node personalises each email from client name and outstanding item list.
Connectors: SharePoint connector, Power Automate (recurrence + condition branching), Generative AI prompt node, Outlook connector
UpsellPath: Add SMS via Twilio for clients who don't respond to email → same tier, higher response rate

Title: Scope Creep Detector
ICBMemberType: Sole trader bookkeeper / small practice owner
WhyItMatters: Scope creep is the silent profit killer for sole-trader bookkeepers — a client asks one extra question, then another, and suddenly the bookkeeper is providing tax advice they're not licensed for and doing work outside the engagement letter for free. This agent checks any client request against the agreed scope before the bookkeeper spends time on it.
Description: Bookkeeper pastes or describes a client request. Agent compares it against the agreed scope of work in the engagement letter and returns one of three verdicts: in scope, out of scope (variation required), or out of scope and outside BAS agent licence (refer to tax agent).
AUContext: AU-specific: BAS agents are licensed under TASA to provide BAS services only — they cannot provide income tax advice, capital gains advice, or estate planning. Scope creep into tax advice territory creates TPB liability. Agent must flag requests that cross into Tax Agent territory (e.g. income tax return advice, CGT calculations, SMSF advice).
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: bookkeeper inputs client request (pasted text or described). Generative AI node with embedded scope template and BAS agent licence boundary rules returns structured verdict: in scope / variation needed / refer to tax agent. Outputs recommended response wording for bookkeeper to send to client.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded BAS agent licence scope rules and TASA boundaries, Copilot Studio topic
UpsellPath: Connect to SharePoint engagement letter store — agent pulls client's actual scope document instead of relying on generic template → Connected Agent (3pts)

Title: BAS Deadline Tracker
ICBMemberType: BAS agent managing multiple client lodgements
WhyItMatters: A BAS agent managing 30 clients across quarterly and monthly lodgement cycles has up to 90 lodgement deadlines per year. Missing even one triggers an ATO Failure to Lodge penalty of $313 per 28 days. A single missed deadline can cost the bookkeeper their client and their professional reputation. This agent tracks every deadline and escalates before it's too late.
Description: Maintains a SharePoint client register with lodgement type (monthly/quarterly), due dates, and completion status. Sends tiered alerts to the bookkeeper at 14 days, 7 days, and 3 days before each deadline. Flags overdue lodgements immediately.
AUContext: AU-specific: Quarterly BAS deadlines — Q1 (Jul–Sep): 28 Oct, Q2 (Oct–Dec): 28 Feb, Q3 (Jan–Mar): 28 Apr, Q4 (Apr–Jun): 28 Jul. BAS agents on ATO lodgement program get extended deadlines (25th of month following). Monthly BAS due 21st of following month. SG quarterly deadlines: 28 Oct, 28 Jan, 28 Apr, 28 Jul.
BuildNotes: SharePoint list: client name, lodgement type, quarter/period, due date, status (not started/in progress/lodged). Power Automate daily recurrence checks days to deadline. Condition nodes: 14 days → Teams or Outlook reminder, 7 days → escalation, 3 days → urgent, overdue → immediate alert. Weekly digest summary sent Monday morning.
Connectors: SharePoint connector, Power Automate (daily recurrence + condition branching), Teams connector, Outlook connector
UpsellPath: Add ATO Online Services for Agents API integration for real-time lodgement confirmation → Connected Agent with custom HTTP connector

Title: BAS Preparation Checklist Agent
WhyItMatters: Every BAS preparation involves the same 12–15 steps: bank reconciliation complete, GST coding reviewed, PAYG withholding reconciled, super calculated, prior period adjustments checked. A sole-trader bookkeeper doing this from memory across 30 clients introduces inconsistency and increases error risk. This agent produces a complete, client-specific BAS prep checklist in 30 seconds.
Description: Bookkeeper inputs client name, quarter, and business type. Agent generates a tailored BAS preparation checklist covering all required reconciliation, coding, and compliance steps for that client — including any state-specific obligations like payroll tax or TPAR if applicable.
AUContext: AU-specific: TPAR (Taxable Payments Annual Report) lodgement required for clients in construction, cleaning, couriers, IT, security, road freight, and government (due 28 Aug annually). Payroll tax thresholds vary by state: NSW $1.2M, VIC $900K, QLD $1.3M, WA $1M, SA $1.5M, ACT $2M. SG rate 12% from 1 July 2025. Agent should ask if client has contractors to flag TPAR obligation.
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: question nodes collect client business type, industry, has employees (Y/N), has contractors (Y/N), quarter. Generative AI node generates tailored checklist with conditional items (TPAR if contractors, payroll tax if wages above threshold, SG reconciliation if employees). Output as formatted Teams message or Outlook email.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded TPAR trigger industries and state payroll tax thresholds, Copilot Studio topic
UpsellPath: Connect to SharePoint client register for automatic pre-population of client details → Connected Agent (3pts)

Title: Superannuation Guarantee Compliance Checker
WhyItMatters: The SG rate reached 12% on 1 July 2025, and wage theft became a criminal offence in January 2025. A bookkeeper who processes payroll with the wrong SG rate, or whose client misses a quarterly payment, is exposing that client to the Superannuation Guarantee Charge — plus their own professional indemnity risk. This agent checks every payroll run for SG compliance before it's processed.
Description: Before a payroll run is finalised, checks that superannuation contributions are calculated at the current SG rate, are allocated to the correct complying fund, and will be paid by the quarterly deadline. Flags any shortfalls, wrong rates, or employees missing fund details.
AUContext: AU-specific: SG rate 12% from 1 July 2025 (previously 11.5%). SG quarterly payment deadlines: 28 Oct, 28 Jan, 28 Apr, 28 Jul. SGC (Superannuation Guarantee Charge) applies if late — calculated on salary and wages, not OTE, which makes it more expensive than the original obligation. Payday Super proposed from 1 July 2026 — contributions will need to align with each pay cycle.
BuildNotes: Bookkeeper inputs payroll run details: pay period, total gross wages, SG amount calculated, employee count. Generative AI node checks: correct SG rate (12% if post-July 2025), payment due date based on quarter, flags any issues. Outputs compliance summary with pass/fail per check. Optional: SharePoint log of each payroll compliance check for PI insurance audit trail.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded SG rate schedule and quarterly deadline table, Copilot Studio topic, SharePoint connector (audit log)
UpsellPath: Connect to Xero Payroll API to pull payroll run data automatically → eliminates manual input, same tier

Title: STP Phase 2 Payroll Category Validator
ICBMemberType: Payroll bookkeeper / BAS agent managing STP
WhyItMatters: STP Phase 2 requires bookkeepers to disaggregate gross pay into specific ATO income type categories — ordinary hours, overtime, bonuses, allowances, termination payments. Many Xero and MYOB setups are technically STP-enabled but still non-compliant because the pay categories are mapped incorrectly. ICB members flag this as one of their biggest client headaches. This agent audits the mapping before it becomes an ATO problem.
Description: Bookkeeper describes their client's pay category setup (or uploads a Xero payroll export). Agent reviews each category against ATO STP Phase 2 income type codes and flags any that are incorrectly mapped, missing, or likely to trigger an ATO data mismatch.
AUContext: AU-specific: STP Phase 2 ATO income type codes: SAL (salary and wages), ALW (allowances), BON (bonuses and commissions), OVT (overtime), LVE (leave payments), ETP (employment termination payments), DIR (directors fees), OSP (other separately disclosed payments). Each must map to the correct ATO code or the ATO data will flag discrepancies. The ATO is actively cross-referencing STP data with income tax returns from FY2025.
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: bookkeeper inputs or pastes pay category list with current ATO mapping. Generative AI node with embedded STP Phase 2 income type code table reviews each category and returns: correct mapping, suggested correction, and ATO reference. Output as structured review table — pass/flag/fix per category.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded STP Phase 2 ATO income type code mapping table, Copilot Studio topic
UpsellPath: Connect to Xero Payroll API to pull pay categories directly → auto-audit without manual input → Connected Agent (3pts)

Title: Modern Award Rate Checker
ICBMemberType: Payroll bookkeeper servicing small business clients
WhyItMatters: From January 2025, wage theft is a criminal offence in Australia. A bookkeeper who helps a client set up payroll at the wrong Award rate — even inadvertently — is potentially implicated in that underpayment. With Annual Wage Reviews every July changing minimum rates across 120+ Modern Awards, staying current is a full-time job on its own. This agent checks rates against the current Award before every payroll setup.
Description: Bookkeeper inputs a client employee's role description, employment type, and industry. Agent identifies the applicable Modern Award, the correct classification level, and the current minimum hourly rate — including any recent Annual Wage Review increases.
AUContext: AU-specific: Fair Work Commission Annual Wage Review typically effective 1 July each year. From 1 July 2025, minimum wage increased. 122 Modern Awards cover most industries — most common for ICB clients: Clerks Award, Retail Award, Restaurant Industry Award, General Retail Industry Award, Hair and Beauty Award, Building and Construction Award. Wage theft criminal offence from 1 January 2025 (Fair Work Legislation Amendment Act 2024).
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: question nodes collect role title, duties, industry, employment type (FT/PT/casual), state. HTTP connector calls Fair Work Commission API for current Award rates. Generative AI node structures output: Award name, classification level, current minimum rate, last updated date, casual loading if applicable. Includes disclaimer to verify with Fair Work Ombudsman.
Connectors: HTTP connector (Fair Work Commission Award rates API), Generative AI prompt node, Copilot Studio topic, Outlook connector (optional — email rate confirmation to bookkeeper)
UpsellPath: Add Annual Wage Review alert — agent monitors FWC for new determinations and notifies bookkeeper when rates update → scheduled Power Automate flow

Title: Fixed Fee Quote Generator
ICBMemberType: Sole trader bookkeeper moving from hourly to fixed fees
WhyItMatters: The ICB 2024 survey showed the shift toward fixed-fee pricing among bookkeepers, but most sole traders still price from gut feel because scoping a new client engagement is hard to do consistently. Underquoting a complex client by even a few hours per month costs thousands annually. This agent generates a structured fixed-fee quote from the client's business profile so the bookkeeper prices confidently every time.
Description: Bookkeeper inputs prospective client details: business structure, transaction volume, employee count, GST status, payroll frequency, software used, services required. Agent generates a structured fixed-fee quote with a monthly range and a clear scope of inclusions and exclusions.
AUContext: AU-specific: ICB national average hourly rate $79.91 for members. Quote must separate BAS agent services (requiring TPB registration) from bookkeeping services (no registration required). Scope must explicitly exclude income tax advice, CGT advice, and other Tax Agent services. GST registration threshold $75,000 annual turnover — include if client is unregistered but near threshold.
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: question nodes collect business structure, monthly transactions (estimate), employees (Y/N + count), payroll frequency, BAS lodgement (quarterly/monthly), Xero or MYOB, catch-up required (Y/N). Generative AI node uses ICB benchmark rates and scope variables to produce structured quote with monthly range, inclusions, exclusions, and engagement conditions.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded ICB pricing benchmarks and BAS agent scope rules, Copilot Studio topic
UpsellPath: Add Outlook connector to send quote directly from agent as a formatted engagement letter draft → Connected Agent (3pts)

Title: ATO Correspondence Interpreter
ICBMemberType: BAS agent fielding client ATO notices
WhyItMatters: Small business clients regularly receive ATO letters, notices, and activity statement reminders that they forward to their bookkeeper with 'what does this mean?' ATO correspondence is written in regulatory language that takes time to interpret. For a sole-trader bookkeeper managing 20+ clients, spending 10 minutes per notice adds up fast. This agent reads the notice and produces a plain-English summary and recommended action in under a minute.
Description: Bookkeeper pastes or uploads ATO correspondence. Agent identifies the notice type, summarises what the ATO is saying in plain English, explains what action is required, by when, and what the consequence of non-action is.
AUContext: AU-specific: Common ATO notice types relevant to bookkeepers — Activity Statement Notice of Assessment, BAS overdue reminder, SGC assessment notice, PAYG withholding discrepancy notice, ATO garnishee notice, ATO debt payment arrangement letter, TFN withholding notice. Agent must note if the notice requires a Tax Agent (not BAS agent) to respond — e.g. income tax assessments must be referred to a tax agent.
BuildNotes: Copilot Studio topic: bookkeeper pastes ATO notice text or uploads PDF. Generative AI node identifies notice type from embedded ATO notice taxonomy, produces: plain-English summary, action required, deadline, consequence of non-action, and whether a tax agent referral is needed. Output via Teams message or Outlook.
Connectors: Generative AI prompt node with embedded ATO notice type taxonomy and BAS agent vs tax agent scope boundary, Copilot Studio topic
UpsellPath: Add SharePoint logging — each interpreted notice archived per client with action status tracked → Connected Agent (3pts)
